title: Building AI Research Agents for Market Analysis
summary: Video covers a multi-agent research workflow for market analysis: a collector agent fetches sources, an analyst agent extracts catalysts and risks, and a synthesizer produces structured briefs. Emphasizes prompt chaining and evidence citation. Also discusses backtesting agent outputs against historical data.

useful_patterns.name: Three-stage research pipeline
useful_patterns.description: Collector → Analyst → Synthesizer. Each stage has bounded input/output and passes structured JSON.
useful_patterns.evidence: We use a three-stage pipeline. The collector fetches and normalizes. The analyst extracts. The synthesizer produces the final brief.

useful_patterns.name: Evidence citation in prompts
useful_patterns.description: Require every claim in analyst output to include a source quote or paraphrase.
useful_patterns.evidence: Every catalyst and risk in our analyst output must cite the source. We put that in the system prompt.

key_claims.claim: Structured prompts reduce hallucination in multi-step research
key_claims.evidence: When we switched to structured output with required evidence fields, our false-positive rate dropped.

key_claims.claim: Backtesting agent outputs improves prompt design
key_claims.evidence: We backtest our briefs against what actually happened. That feedback loop changed how we write the synthesizer prompt.

hype_warnings.why_flagged: No methodology, sample size, or time period specified

build_ideas.idea: Adapt three-stage pipeline for Research Swarm: collector already exists; extractor and evaluator map to analyst; archivist maps to synthesizer
build_ideas.why_it_matters: Validates our existing role split; suggests we add explicit evidence fields to prompts
build_ideas.status_note: Align with current extractor_prompt.md evidence requirements

build_ideas.idea: Backtest archived entries: compare extracted catalysts to subsequent events
build_ideas.why_it_matters: Could improve evaluator and archivist prompts over time
build_ideas.status_note: Requires event data and time-series; out of scope for v1

module_mapping.relevance_reason: Pipeline structure and evidence citation align with extractor/evaluator/archivist roles
Structured brief format and catalyst/risk extraction could inform research brief design

open_questions: What backtest methodology did they use? No details in transcript.
How do they handle conflicting sources in the analyst stage?
